title: "Introducing our people: they make us what we are.", Newspaper Clippings

title: "Republic expands bar operation in Gary", Newspaper Clippings
subject: Newspaper; Occupations; Textual records
creator: Unknown Creator
description: Newspaper clippings from the Post-Tribune, January 1976, speaking on the new multi-million dollar cold finished steel bar mill in Gary, run by Republic Steel Corporation. The article talks on the capabilities of the mill, some of the machines they have in-house, and gives a brief history of the ownership.

title: "Strikes harsh blow to Gary economy", Newspaper Clippings
subject: Newspaper; Occupations; Steel; Textual records
creator: James B. Lane
description: Newspaper clippings from the Post-Tribune, September 1975, covering the local steel industry's strikes and labor unions and the effect they are having on the economy.

title: "Problems, progress for Inland Steel", Newspaper Clippings
subject: Newspaper; Occupations; Steel; Textual records
creator: Unknown Creator
description: Newspaper clippings from the Post-Tribune, January 1976, discussing the challenges faced by local steel manufacturer "Inland Steel", citing issues such as labor shortages, rising costs of operation, and restricted access to raw resources. Also included are environmentally friendly initiatives and a $1B+ expansion program.

title: "'76 steel outlook is good", Newspaper Clippings
subject: Newspaper; Occupations; Steel; Textual records
creator: Frederick C. Langenberg
description: Newspaper clippings from the Post-Tribune, January 1976, displaying sentiments from then-president of the American Iron and Steel Institute, Frederick C. Langenberg. Langenberg explains their belief in the bright future of domestic steel. A suggestion is also made that efforts to reduce pollutants at steel mills may actually increase net-production of pollutants into the atmosphere.

title: "Gate City new steel tenant", Newspaper Clippings
subject: Newspaper; Occupations; Steel; Textual records
creator: Unknown Creator
description: Newspaper clippings from the Post-Tribune, January 1976, discussing the Gate City Steel mill in Gary. The column speaks about the prosperity of the mill, the locations of some of the other Gate City mills, and new developments at the mill.

title: "See Steel In Action!", Newspaper Clippings
subject: Newspaper; Occupations; Notices & Advertisements; Steel; Textual records
creator: Unknown Creator

title: "Meet the Steelmaking Pros of Youngstown", Newspaper Clippings
subject: Newspaper; Occupations; Steel; Textual records
creator: Unknown Creator

title: "U.S. Steel Gary Works Establishes World Record", Newspaper Clippings
subject: Newspaper; Occupations; Steel; Textual records
creator: Unknown Creator
description: Newspaper clippings from the Times of Northwest Indiana, April 1976, celebrating the world-record 300-millionth ton of steel produced. It was a record set that day by the local USS Gary Works steel mill.

title: "Gary Works will close three polluting batteries", Newspaper Clippings
subject: Newspaper; Occupations; Steel; Textual records
creator: Unknown Creator
description: Newspaper clippings from the Post-Tribune, November 1975, announcing the upcoming deprecations of batteries at the USS Gary Works steel mill. The closures are being done to reduce pollution. Another column discusses the juxtaposition of both pride and shame Northwest Indiana feels towards its steel mills, both for its pollution output and its contribution to the nation's economy.

title: Collection of Newspaper Clippings Showcasing Booming Steel Industry in Gary
subject: Newspaper; Occupations; Steel; Textual records

title: "Ex-Gary man first black Steelworker executive", Newspaper Clippings
subject: Newspaper; Occupations; Steel; Textual records
creator: Unknown Creator
description: Newspaper clippings from the Post-Tribune, September 1976, celebrating the first-ever black executive in the steel industry, Leon Lynch. Lynch, a Gary native, started work in the industry at the Youngstown steel mill in 1956 and now serves as an executive on the worker's union in Memphis, Tennessee.
